risk_if_not_implemented: Without Auditing Remote Maintenance, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Non-Console Administrative Access
compensating_control_1.description: Cryptographic mechanisms exist to protect the confidentiality and integrity of non-console administrative access.
compensating_control_1.justification: Non-Console Administrative Access (CRY-06) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Auditing Remote Maintenance (MNT-05.1) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
compensating_control_2.description:
Automated mechanisms exist to enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for:
(1) Remote network access; 
(2) Third-party Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS); and/ or
(3) Non-console access to critical TAAS that store, transmit and/or process sensitive/regulated data.
compensating_control_2.justification: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) (IAC-06) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Auditing Remote Maintenance (MNT-05.1) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
